description: Audience Engine — Platform-layer segment registry and filter evaluation service. Covers: @platform/filter-engine pure-function evaluator package, Audience Engine REST service (segment CRUD, named/inline batch evaluate, snapshot management, membership check), BullMQ snapshot cleanup workers, and @platform/audience-ui React component package.

userStories.title: @platform/filter-engine — package scaffold + types + base operators + tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a zero-dependency pure-function TypeScript package that evaluates a generic JSON filter tree against an entity object so that both the Audience Engine and Automation Engine can share the same filter evaluation logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `packages/@platform/filter-engine/package.json`: name `@platform/filter-engine`, version `0.1.0`, type `module`, main `dist/index.js`, types `dist/index.d.ts`, engines `{ node: '>=24' }`, scripts `{ build: 'tsc', test: 'vitest run', typecheck: 'tsc --noEmit' }`. NO runtime dependencies. devDependencies: `typescript ^5.0.0`, `@types/node ^22.0.0`, `vitest ^2.0.0`.

Create `packages/@platform/filter-engine/tsconfig.json`: compilerOptions `{ target: 'ES2022', module: 'NodeNext', moduleResolution: 'NodeNext', strict: true, outDir: 'dist', rootDir: 'src', declaration: true, declarationMap: true, sourceMap: true, esModuleInterop: true, skipLibCheck: true }`, include `['src/**/*', 'test/**/*']`, exclude `['node_modules', 'dist']`.
Create `packages/@platform/filter-engine/vitest.config.ts`: export default `{ test: { passWithNoTests: true } }`.

Create `packages/@platform/filter-engine/src/types.ts`: export `ConditionNode` type `{ field: string; op: 'eq'|'neq'|'in'|'not_in'|'gt'|'gte'|'lt'|'lte'|'contains'|'exists'|'not_exists'|'within_last'|'not_within_last'|'before'|'after'|'date_range'; value?: unknown }`, export `GroupNode` type `{ op: 'AND'|'OR'; conditions: FilterNode[] }`, export `NotNode` type `{ op: 'NOT'; condition: FilterNode }`, export `FilterNode` type `ConditionNode | GroupNode | NotNode`, export `EvalContext` type `{ now: Date }`. Export type guards: `isGroup(node: FilterNode): node is GroupNode`, `isNot(node: FilterNode): node is NotNode`, `isLeaf(node: FilterNode): node is ConditionNode`.

Create `packages/@platform/filter-engine/src/operators/base.ts`: export function `evaluateBase(node: ConditionNode, entity: Record<string, unknown>): boolean`. Resolve field value by dot-notation path (e.g. `address.city` → `entity.address?.city`). A missing/undefined field: `not_exists` returns true, all other operators return false. Operators: `exists` (field is not undefined/null), `not_exists`, `eq` (===), `neq` (!==), `in` (Array.isArray(value) && value.includes(fieldValue)), `not_in`, `gt`/`gte`/`lt`/`lte` (Number coercion), `contains` (string: fieldValue.includes(value); array: fieldValue.includes(value)). Throw `Error('Unknown base operator: <op>')` for unrecognised ops.

Create `packages/@platform/filter-engine/src/evaluate.ts`: export `evaluate(filter: FilterNode, entity: Record<string, unknown>, context?: EvalContext): boolean`. If `isNot(filter)`: return `!evaluate(filter.condition, entity, context)`. If `isGroup(filter)` AND: short-circuit all sub-conditions. If `isGroup(filter)` OR: short-circuit any sub-condition. Otherwise call `evaluateBase(filter, entity)` — but first check if the op is a temporal op (`within_last`, `not_within_last`, `before`, `after`, `date_range`) and throw `Error('Temporal operator requires EvalContext')` if `context` is undefined.
Create `packages/@platform/filter-engine/src/index.ts`: re-export `evaluate` from `./evaluate.js`, all types from `./types.js`.

Create `packages/@platform/filter-engine/test/base.test.ts` with Vitest tests covering: `eq` match, `eq` miss, `neq`, `in` match, `in` miss, `not_in`, `gt`/`gte`/`lt`/`lte` numeric comparison, `contains` on string, `contains` on array, `exists` present field, `not_exists` missing field, `exists` on null returns false, AND group (all match), AND group (one fails → false), OR group (one matches → true), OR group (all fail → false), NOT node inverts result, nested AND inside OR, missing field with `eq` returns false, missing field with `not_exists` returns true, dot-notation nested path resolved correctly, unknown op throws descriptive error, temporal op without context throws descriptive error.
Run `npm install` from `packages/@platform/filter-engine/`
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: This is a pure-function package — no Fastify, no Knex, no Redis. Zero runtime dependencies. The evaluate.ts file contains the routing logic (AND/OR/NOT dispatch + temporal guard), while base.ts contains only the leaf operator evaluation. Dot-notation field resolution: split on '.', reduce over entity object. A null intermediate node (e.g. entity.address is null when resolving address.city) should return undefined (not throw).

userStories.title: @platform/filter-engine — temporal operators + full test coverage
userStories.description: As a developer, I need temporal filter operators (within_last, not_within_last, before, after, date_range) in the filter-engine package so that the Audience Engine can segment entities by date fields.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `packages/@platform/filter-engine/src/operators/temporal.ts`: export `evaluateTemporal(node: ConditionNode, entity: Record<string, unknown>, context: EvalContext): boolean`. Resolve field value via same dot-notation resolver as base.ts. If field value is undefined/null, return false for all temporal operators. Parse field value to Date (accept ISO 8601 string or Date). Operators: `within_last` — `fieldDate >= context.now - N` (inclusive; value shape `{ amount: number, unit: 'days'|'hours' }`); `not_within_last` — `fieldDate < context.now - N`; `before` — `fieldDate < new Date(value as string)`; `after` — `fieldDate > new Date(value as string)`; `date_range` — `fieldDate >= new Date(start) && fieldDate <= new Date(end)` (value shape `{ start: string, end: string }`). Throw `Error('Unknown temporal operator: <op>')` for unrecognised ops.

Update `packages/@platform/filter-engine/src/evaluate.ts`: import `evaluateTemporal` from `./operators/temporal.js`. In the leaf branch, check if the op is temporal before calling `evaluateBase`. Temporal ops: `within_last`, `not_within_last`, `before`, `after`, `date_range`. If temporal and `context` is undefined, throw `Error('EvalContext with { now: Date } is required for temporal operators')`. Otherwise call `evaluateTemporal(filter, entity, context)`.

Create `packages/@platform/filter-engine/test/temporal.test.ts` with Vitest tests covering: `within_last N days` — field exactly N days ago is INCLUDED (boundary inclusive, `fieldDate === now - N` → true); `within_last` — field N+1 days ago → false; `not_within_last` — field N days ago → false (boundary); `not_within_last` — field N+1 days ago → true; `within_last N hours` boundary inclusive; `before` — field before date → true; `before` — field on exact date → false (strictly less than); `after` — field after date → true; `date_range` — field within range → true; `date_range` — field on start boundary → true; `date_range` — field outside range → false; missing field (undefined) → false for all temporal ops; context.now correctly used (inject specific `now` date); unknown temporal op throws; calling temporal op without EvalContext throws.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: The `within_last` boundary semantics from spec: `timestamp >= now - N` (inclusive). So a field exactly N days ago (to the millisecond) matches `within_last N days`. For day arithmetic: subtract `amount * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000` milliseconds from `context.now.getTime()`. The `before` operator is strictly less-than (not <=). The `date_range` operator is inclusive on both ends (>= start && <= end).

userStories.title: Audience Engine — service scaffold + DB schema + migrations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Audience Engine service project structure, database schema, Fastify bootstrap, and liveness health check so that subsequent stories can build route handlers and services on a solid foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `apps/platform/audience/package.json`: name `@platform/audience`, version `0.1.0`, type `module`, engines `{ node: '>=24' }`, scripts `{ build: 'tsc', dev: 'tsx watch src/index.ts', start: 'node dist/index.js', test: 'vitest run', typecheck: 'tsc --noEmit' }`. dependencies: `fastify ^5.0.0`, `@fastify/sensible ^6.0.0`, `@sinclair/typebox ^0.34.0`, `knex ^3.0.0`, `pg ^8.0.0`, `ioredis ^5.0.0`, `bullmq ^5.0.0`, `@platform/filter-engine file:../../../packages/@platform/filter-engine`, `@ortho/logger file:../../../packages/@ortho/logger`. devDependencies: `typescript ^5.0.0`, `@types/node ^22.0.0`, `@types/pg ^8.0.0`, `tsx ^4.0.0`, `vitest ^2.0.0`.

Create `apps/platform/audience/tsconfig.json`: same compilerOptions as email service (target ES2022, module NodeNext, moduleResolution NodeNext, strict true, outDir dist, rootDir src, declaration true, declarationMap true, sourceMap true, esModuleInterop true, skipLibCheck true). include `['src/**/*']`, exclude `['node_modules', 'dist']`.
Create `apps/platform/audience/vitest.config.ts`: `export default { test: { passWithNoTests: true } }`.

Create `apps/platform/audience/src/env.ts`: TypeBox-validated env parsing for `DATABASE_URL` (string), `REDIS_URL` (string), `PORT` (optional integer, default 3000). Follow email service env.ts pattern exactly.
Create `apps/platform/audience/src/db.ts`: export `createDb(databaseUrl: string)` returning Knex instance with `client: 'pg'`. Re-export `Knex` type. Same pattern as email service db.ts.
Create `apps/platform/audience/src/types.d.ts`: Fastify module augmentation adding `db: Knex` and `redis: Redis` to FastifyInstance (no eventBus — audience engine has no EventBridge).

Create `apps/platform/audience/src/routes/health.ts`: export `healthRoutes(app: FastifyInstance)`. Route `GET /health` always returns `200 { status: 'ok' }`. No connectivity checks (spec: liveness only).

Create `apps/platform/audience/src/app.ts`: export `buildApp(db: Knex, redis: Redis): Promise<FastifyInstance>`. Register `@fastify/sensible`, decorate with db and redis, register `onClose` hook closing redis connection. Register `healthRoutes`. Return app.

Create `apps/platform/audience/src/index.ts`: parse env, create db via `createDb(env.DATABASE_URL)`, create redis via `new Redis(env.REDIS_URL, { maxRetriesPerRequest: null })`, call `buildApp(db, redis)`, listen on `env.PORT` host `0.0.0.0`. SIGTERM handler: close app, destroy db, disconnect redis.

Create `apps/platform/audience/migrations/001_initial_schema.ts` (Knex migration). `up` creates: (1) `audience_segments` table: `id` uuid PK default `gen_random_uuid()`, `name` text NOT NULL, `status` text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft', `active_version` integer nullable, `current_version` integer NOT NULL DEFAULT 1, `created_by` uuid nullable, `created_at` timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), `updated_at` timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(). (2) `audience_segment_versions` table: `id` uuid PK default `gen_random_uuid()`, `segment_id` uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES audience_segments(id), `version` integer NOT NULL, `filter` jsonb NOT NULL, `created_by` uuid nullable, `created_at` timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(). Add UNIQUE constraint on `(segment_id, version)`. (3) `audience_snapshots` table: `id` uuid PK (no default — caller-supplied), `segment_id` uuid nullable REFERENCES audience_segments(id), `segment_version` integer nullable, `filter_snapshot` jsonb NOT NULL, `status` text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'accumulating', `matched_count` integer NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, `expires_at` timestamptz NOT NULL, `created_by` uuid nullable, `created_at` timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(). (4) `audience_snapshot_members` table: `snapshot_id` uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES audience_snapshots(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, `entity_id` text NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (snapshot_id, entity_id). Indexes: `CREATE INDEX ON audience_segments (status)`, `CREATE INDEX ON audience_snapshots (segment_id)`, `CREATE INDEX ON audience_snapshots (expires_at)`. `down` drops all four tables in reverse order.
Run `npm install` from `apps/platform/audience/`
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: No EventBridge / event-bus dependency — the audience engine is purely synchronous REST. No @ortho/event-bus in dependencies. Redis is only for BullMQ snapshot cleanup workers (added in US-010). The health endpoint is intentionally liveness-only (always 200) — this differs from the email service which does DB/Redis connectivity checks. The audience_snapshots.id is caller-supplied (no gen_random_uuid() default). audience_snapshot_members uses composite PK. Use `knex.raw('gen_random_uuid()')` as default for segment/version tables.

userStories.title: Audience Engine — segment repository with 30s cache + filter-evaluator wrapper
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a SegmentRepository with per-instance in-memory 30s TTL cache and a FilterEvaluator wrapper around @platform/filter-engine so that membership checks are fast and temporal operator context is correctly injected.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `apps/platform/audience/src/services/segment-repository.ts`: export interface `SegmentWithFilter { id: string; name: string; status: string; active_version: number | null; current_version: number; filter: unknown | null }`. Export class `SegmentRepository`. Constructor takes `db: Knex`. Private cache: `Map<string, { value: SegmentWithFilter; expiresAt: number }>` with TTL_MS = 30_000. Method `getActiveWithFilter(segmentId: string): Promise<SegmentWithFilter | null>`: check cache first (if hit and `Date.now() < expiresAt`, return cached value); on cache miss, query `SELECT s.*, v.filter FROM audience_segments s LEFT JOIN audience_segment_versions v ON v.segment_id = s.id AND v.version = s.active_version WHERE s.id = ?` — if not found or status !== 'active' or active_version IS NULL, return null; store in cache with `expiresAt = Date.now() + TTL_MS`; return result. Method `invalidate(segmentId: string): void` removes entry from cache.

Create `apps/platform/audience/src/services/filter-evaluator.ts`: import `{ evaluate }` from `@platform/filter-engine`. Export class `FilterEvaluator`. Method `evaluate(filter: unknown, entity: Record<string, unknown>): boolean`: call `evaluate(filter as FilterNode, entity, { now: new Date() })` and return result. Wrap in try/catch — if `evaluate` throws (unknown operator, malformed node), rethrow as-is. This wrapper always passes a fresh `EvalContext` with current time.
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The segment repository cache is per-ECS-instance (not Redis). 30s TTL means a newly activated segment or a disabled segment may be stale for up to 30s — this is an accepted design trade-off per the spec. The cache is a simple Map with expiry check on read, no eviction timer. The filter-evaluator wrapper always passes `{ now: new Date() }` context so temporal operators work correctly. The `filter` column comes from the LEFT JOIN on audience_segment_versions at the active_version.

userStories.title: Audience Engine — segment CRUD routes (create, update, list, get)
userStories.description: As a caller (Campaign Service, marketing staff via audience-ui), I need REST endpoints to create named segment definitions, update draft filters with correct versioning, list segments, and retrieve segment details.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `apps/platform/audience/src/routes/segments.ts`: export Fastify plugin `segmentRoutes`. Register in `buildApp()` in app.ts (no prefix — routes include full paths like `/audiences/segments`).

Create helper `jsonApiError(status: number, code: string, title: string, detail?: string)` in the same file or a shared errors module: returns `{ errors: [{ status: String(status), code, title, detail }] }`.

Create `apps/platform/audience/src/repositories/segments.repository.ts`: export class `SegmentsRepository` (constructor takes `db: Knex`). Methods: `create(data: { id: string; name: string; created_by?: string | null }): Promise<AudienceSegment>` (INSERT into audience_segments returning *), `createVersion(data: { id: string; segment_id: string; version: number; filter: unknown; created_by?: string | null }): Promise<void>` (INSERT into audience_segment_versions), `findById(id: string): Promise<AudienceSegment | null>`, `findVersionRow(segmentId: string, version: number): Promise<AudienceSegmentVersion | null>`, `updateCurrentVersionFilter(segmentId: string, version: number, filter: unknown): Promise<void>` (UPDATE audience_segment_versions SET filter=? WHERE segment_id=? AND version=?), `incrementVersion(segmentId: string): Promise<number>` (UPDATE audience_segments SET current_version = current_version + 1, updated_at = now() WHERE id=? RETURNING current_version), `updateStatus(segmentId: string, status: string, activeVersion?: number): Promise<AudienceSegment | null>` (UPDATE returning *), `list(filters: { status?: string[] }, pagination: { limit: number; offset: number }): Promise<{ items: AudienceSegment[]; total: number }>`. TypeBox schemas for AudienceSegment and AudienceSegmentVersion types.

Route `POST /audiences/segments`: TypeBox body `{ name: string; filter: unknown }`. Both required. Validate `filter` is a non-null object (basic structural check — not full DSL validation; defer DSL validation). Generate `segment_id = crypto.randomUUID()`, `version_id = crypto.randomUUID()`. In a single Knex transaction: INSERT segment (id, name, created_by from JWT sub if present, default current_version=1, status='draft'), INSERT version (id=version_id, segment_id, version=1, filter, created_by). Return `201 { segment_id, version: 1, status: 'draft' }`. Return `400 jsonApiError` if `filter` is absent or not an object.

Route `PUT /audiences/segments/:id`: TypeBox body `{ filter: unknown }`. Load segment by id → 404 if not found. Versioning logic: if `active_version IS NULL` OR `current_version > active_version` (draft never activated) → call `updateCurrentVersionFilter(id, current_version, filter)`, return `200 { segment_id, version: current_version, status: segment.status }`. If `current_version === active_version` (current version was activated) → call `incrementVersion(id)` to get new `current_version`, then INSERT new version row (segment_id, version=new_current_version, filter, created_by), update `updated_at`, return `200 { segment_id, version: new_current_version, status: 'draft' }`. After updating filter, call `segmentRepository.invalidate(id)` to clear cache. Return `400` if `filter` is absent or not an object.

Route `GET /audiences/segments`: query params `limit` (integer, default 100, max 1000), `offset` (integer, default 0), `status` (optional string — comma-separated values e.g. `active,draft`). Parse `status` param into string array by splitting on comma. Call `SegmentsRepository.list({ status: statusArray }, { limit, offset })`. Return `200 { items: [...], total: N }`. Items: `{ segment_id, name, status, active_version, current_version, updated_at }`.

Route `GET /audiences/segments/:id`: load segment by id → 404 if not found. If status=active: load filter from active_version row and return `{ segment_id, name, status, active_version, current_version, filter }`. If status=draft: return `{ segment_id, name, status, active_version: null, current_version, filter: null }`. If status=disabled: return `{ segment_id, name, status, active_version, current_version, filter: null }`.
Register `segmentRoutes` in `buildApp()` — ensure `db` and a `SegmentRepository` instance are accessible in route handlers (decorate app with `segmentRepository` or instantiate in plugin scope).
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: JWT sub extraction: check `request.headers.authorization` or use a helper. For now (no auth middleware), default `created_by` to null if no JWT is present — don't fail requests without auth. The spec says `created_by` is auto-populated from JWT sub; in practice without @ortho/auth-middleware wired up, just pass null. The versioning rule: compare `current_version` and `active_version` on the loaded segment row. `active_version IS NULL` = never activated = overwrite draft. `current_version === active_version` = activated version exists = create new version. Use crypto.randomUUID() from Node built-ins (no uuid package needed). The SegmentRepository (with cache) from US-004 is separate from the SegmentsRepository (raw DB ops) here — instantiate both in app.ts and pass to routes.

userStories.title: Audience Engine — segment lifecycle routes (activate, disable)
userStories.description: As a Marketing Manager, I need to activate a draft segment (making it available for evaluate calls) and disable a segment (making it unavailable) so that segment lifecycle is controlled.
acceptanceCriteria: Add route `POST /audiences/segments/:id/activate` to `segment-routes.ts`: load segment by id → 404 if not found. Verify a version row exists for `current_version` → 400 with `jsonApiError(400, 'NO_FILTER_VERSION', 'No filter version for current_version', ...)` if missing. UPDATE audience_segments SET status='active', active_version=current_version, updated_at=now() WHERE id=?. Call `segmentRepository.invalidate(id)`. Return `200 { segment_id, active_version: current_version, status: 'active' }`.

Add route `POST /audiences/segments/:id/disable` to `segment-routes.ts`: load segment by id → 404 if not found. UPDATE audience_segments SET status='disabled', updated_at=now() WHERE id=?. Call `segmentRepository.invalidate(id)`. Return `200 { segment_id, status: 'disabled' }`.
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The spec mentions Marketing Manager role check for activate and disable (403 if not that role). Since @ortho/auth-middleware is not yet wired up, skip the role check for now and note it as a TODO comment. The spec also says activate returns 403 if not Marketing Manager — add TODO comment. The key functional behavior (version promotion, cache invalidation) must be correct. Cache invalidation after activate/disable ensures the membership check endpoint picks up the new state within TTL.

userStories.title: Audience Engine — membership check route (POST /audiences/segments/:id/check)
userStories.description: As the Automation Engine or a product service, I want to synchronously check whether a single entity matches a named segment's active filter so that I can branch workflow logic based on audience membership.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `apps/platform/audience/src/routes/check.ts`: export Fastify plugin `checkRoutes`. Register in `buildApp()`.

Route `POST /audiences/segments/:id/check`: TypeBox body `{ entity: Record<string, unknown> }`. Use `SegmentRepository.getActiveWithFilter(id)` (cache-backed) to load segment + filter. If null (no active version or disabled): return `404 jsonApiError(404, 'SEGMENT_NOT_FOUND', ...)`. Pass `entity` to `FilterEvaluator.evaluate(filter, entity)`. Return `200 { matches: boolean, segment_id: string, segment_version: number }`. `segment_version` comes from the cached entry's `active_version`.
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: No snapshot created, no DB write on the hot path. The SegmentRepository from US-004 is the cache-backed one. The FilterEvaluator from US-004 injects `now` automatically. If the filter-engine throws (malformed filter stored in DB), let the error bubble up to Fastify's error handler as a 500. The 30s cache staleness (disabled segment still returns results for up to 30s) is accepted per spec.

userStories.title: Audience Engine — snapshot manager + named segment batch evaluate route
userStories.description: As the Campaign Service, I want to submit entity data in batches to evaluate a named segment and accumulate matched entity IDs into a snapshot so that I can drive a bulk send loop.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `apps/platform/audience/src/repositories/snapshots.repository.ts`: export class `SnapshotsRepository` (constructor takes `db: Knex`). Methods: `createIfNotExists(data: { id: string; segment_id: string | null; segment_version: number | null; filter_snapshot: unknown; expires_at: Date; created_by: string | null }): Promise<void>` — INSERT INTO audience_snapshots ... ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING; `findById(id: string): Promise<AudienceSnapshot | null>`; `addMembers(snapshotId: string, entityIds: string[]): Promise<number>` — INSERT INTO audience_snapshot_members (snapshot_id, entity_id) SELECT $snapshotId, unnest($entityIds::text[]) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING — returns count of actually inserted rows; `incrementMatchedCount(snapshotId: string, delta: number): Promise<void>` — UPDATE SET matched_count = matched_count + delta; `getMatchedCount(snapshotId: string): Promise<number>`; `seal(snapshotId: string): Promise<void>` — UPDATE SET status='ready'; `findMembers(snapshotId: string, limit: number, offset: number): Promise<string[]>` — SELECT entity_id ... ORDER BY entity_id; `validateSegmentId(snapshotId: string, expectedSegmentId: string): Promise<boolean>` — loads snapshot and checks segment_id matches.

Create `apps/platform/audience/src/services/snapshot-manager.ts`: export class `SnapshotManager`. Constructor takes `SnapshotsRepository`, `FilterEvaluator`, and `enqueueCleanup: (snapshotId: string, delayMs: number) => Promise<void>` (injected callback). Method `processBatch(params: ProcessBatchParams): Promise<ProcessBatchResult>`. ProcessBatchParams: `{ snapshotId: string; segmentId: string | null; segmentVersion: number | null; filterSnapshot: unknown; entities: Array<{ entity_id: string; [key: string]: unknown }>; done: boolean; createdBy: string | null }`. Logic: (1) Create snapshot row if not exists via `createIfNotExists` (idempotent — ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING); (2) Validate: if existing snapshot's segment_id doesn't match `segmentId`, throw typed `SegmentMismatchError`; (3) Filter entities: run `FilterEvaluator.evaluate(filterSnapshot, entity)` on each entity, collect `matchedIds`; (4) Check total cap: `getMatchedCount(snapshotId) + matchedIds.length > 100_000` → throw `SnapshotSizeExceededError` (no partial write); (5) `addMembers(snapshotId, matchedIds)` and `incrementMatchedCount(snapshotId, matchedIds.length)`; (6) If `done`: `seal(snapshotId)` and `enqueueCleanup(snapshotId, 48 * 60 * 60 * 1000)`; (7) Return `{ snapshotId, matchedCount: newTotal, status: done ? 'ready' : 'accumulating' }`.
Create `apps/platform/audience/src/routes/evaluate.ts`: export Fastify plugin `evaluateRoutes`. Register in `buildApp()`.

Route `POST /audiences/segments/:id/evaluate`: TypeBox body `{ snapshot_id: string; entities: unknown[]; done: boolean }`. Validations: `snapshot_id` required (400 if absent); entities.length <= 1000 (413 if exceeded); load segment by id (404 if no active version or disabled). Check if snapshot exists and status is 'ready' → 400 with `SNAPSHOT_ALREADY_SEALED`. Call `snapshotManager.processBatch({ snapshotId: body.snapshot_id, segmentId: params.id, segmentVersion: segment.active_version, filterSnapshot: segment.filter, entities: body.entities, done: body.done, createdBy: null })`. Catch `SegmentMismatchError` → 400. Catch `SnapshotSizeExceededError` → 400 with code `SNAPSHOT_SIZE_EXCEEDED`. Return `{ snapshot_id, matched_count, status }`.
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The `addMembers` query uses unnest to efficiently insert multiple rows in one query: `knex.raw('INSERT INTO audience_snapshot_members (snapshot_id, entity_id) SELECT ?, unnest(?::text[]) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING', [snapshotId, entityIds])` — returns count by checking rowCount. The `enqueueCleanup` callback is injected to keep snapshot-manager free of BullMQ dependency (BullMQ wired in US-010). In US-008, pass a no-op stub for `enqueueCleanup` in app.ts until US-010 wires it properly. The `SegmentMismatchError` and `SnapshotSizeExceededError` are custom error classes (extend Error, set a name property).

userStories.title: Audience Engine — inline evaluate route + snapshot retrieval route
userStories.description: As a Campaign Service or automation tool, I want to evaluate an inline filter (no stored segment) against entity data, optionally accumulating into a snapshot, and retrieve snapshot members for driving send loops.
acceptanceCriteria: Add route `POST /audiences/evaluate` to `evaluate-routes.ts`: TypeBox body `{ snapshot_id?: string; filter: unknown; entities: unknown[]; done: boolean; snapshot: boolean }`. `snapshot` defaults to false. Validations: entities.length <= 1000 → 413; if `snapshot: false` and `done: false` → 400 (`INVALID_REQUEST: snapshot:false requires done:true`); if `snapshot: true` and `snapshot_id` absent → 400 (`MISSING_SNAPSHOT_ID`); filter required (400 if absent or not object). If `snapshot: false` (and `done: true`): run `FilterEvaluator.evaluate(filter, entity)` on each entity inline, collect matchedIds, return `200 { matched_count: matchedIds.length, entity_ids: matchedIds }` — no DB writes. If `snapshot: true`: if snapshot exists and its `filter_snapshot` !== JSON.stringify of incoming filter → 400 (`FILTER_MISMATCH`); call `snapshotManager.processBatch({ snapshotId: body.snapshot_id, segmentId: null, segmentVersion: null, filterSnapshot: body.filter, entities: body.entities, done: body.done, createdBy: null })`. Catch `SnapshotSizeExceededError` → 400 `SNAPSHOT_SIZE_EXCEEDED`. Return `{ snapshot_id, matched_count, status }`.
Create `apps/platform/audience/src/routes/snapshots.ts`: export Fastify plugin `snapshotRoutes`. Register in `buildApp()`.

Route `GET /audiences/snapshots/:snapshot_id`: query params `limit` (integer, default 1000, max 10000), `offset` (integer, default 0). Load snapshot via `SnapshotsRepository.findById`. If not found → 404. Load entity_ids via `SnapshotsRepository.findMembers(snapshot_id, limit, offset)`. Return `200 { snapshot_id, segment_id: snapshot.segment_id | null, segment_version: snapshot.segment_version | null, status, matched_count, expires_at, entity_ids }`.
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Filter mismatch check for inline snapshot: load the existing snapshot row's `filter_snapshot` and compare against the incoming `filter` using `JSON.stringify(existing) !== JSON.stringify(incoming)`. This is a simple string comparison — not deep equal — consistent with how the filter was stored as jsonb and retrieved. The inline `snapshot: false` path never writes any DB rows — it's pure in-memory filter evaluation. The spec says `snapshot_id` is null for inline one-off snapshots in the GET response.

userStories.title: Audience Engine — BullMQ snapshot cleanup workers (delayed job + hourly safety-net)
userStories.description: As the system, I need two BullMQ workers to delete expired audience snapshots: a per-snapshot delayed job fired at creation time (48h delay), and an hourly safety-net sweep for any snapshots whose primary job was lost.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `apps/platform/audience/src/services/snapshot-cleanup.ts`: export function `createSnapshotCleanupQueue(connection: Redis): Queue` — creates BullMQ Queue named `audience-snapshot-cleanup`. Export function `enqueueSnapshotCleanup(queue: Queue, snapshotId: string, delayMs: number): Promise<void>` — adds a job `{ snapshotId }` with `delay: delayMs`, `attempts: 1`. Export function `createSnapshotCleanupWorker(connection: Redis, db: Knex): Worker` — processes `audience-snapshot-cleanup` queue; job handler: `DELETE FROM audience_snapshots WHERE id = ?` (no-op if already deleted — 0 rows deleted is not an error); log `info` with `{ snapshotId, deleted: rowCount > 0 }`. Worker concurrency: 1.

Create `apps/platform/audience/src/services/snapshot-cleanup-sweep.ts`: export function `createCleanupSweepWorker(connection: Redis, db: Knex): Worker` — creates a BullMQ Worker processing queue named `audience-snapshot-cleanup-sweep`. On init, adds a repeatable job: `queue.add('sweep', {}, { repeat: { every: 60 * 60 * 1000 } })`. Job handler: `DELETE FROM audience_snapshots WHERE expires_at < NOW()`, log count of deleted rows.

Update `apps/platform/audience/src/app.ts`: accept `cleanupQueue: Queue` as third parameter to `buildApp(db, redis, cleanupQueue)`. Register the `enqueueCleanup` callback on `snapshotManager` as `(snapshotId, delayMs) => enqueueSnapshotCleanup(cleanupQueue, snapshotId, delayMs)`. Register cleanup worker and sweep worker created with the same redis connection in `onReady` hook (call `worker.run()` or rely on autorun). Register `onClose` hook to close cleanupQueue.

Update `apps/platform/audience/src/index.ts`: create `cleanupQueue` via `createSnapshotCleanupQueue(redis)`, pass to `buildApp`. Start `createSnapshotCleanupWorker(redis, db)` and `createCleanupSweepWorker(redis, db)` workers.
Backfill US-008: replace the no-op `enqueueCleanup` stub in `snapshotManager` with the real queue-backed callback.
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: BullMQ Worker with `autorun: true` (default) starts processing immediately on instantiation. The sweep worker adds its own repeatable job on first run — this is idempotent (BullMQ deduplicates repeatable jobs by key). The `DELETE FROM audience_snapshots WHERE id = ?` cascades to `audience_snapshot_members` via ON DELETE CASCADE — no separate delete needed. Workers use the same Redis connection as the queue. Follow email service worker pattern: `new Worker('queue-name', async (job) => { ... }, { connection })`. The `cleanupQueue` parameter threads through app.ts because snapshotManager needs the enqueue callback — this avoids importing BullMQ directly in snapshot-manager.ts.

userStories.title: Audience Engine — unit tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need focused unit tests for the segment repository cache, filter evaluator wrapper, and snapshot manager batch logic so that isolated behavior is verified without a database or Redis.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `apps/platform/audience/test/unit/segment-repository.test.ts`: (a) cache hit: call `getActiveWithFilter` twice with same id — DB query called only once, second call returns cached value; (b) cache miss after TTL: manipulate `expiresAt` to past time, verify DB queried again; (c) non-active segment (status='draft') returns null; (d) segment with no active_version returns null; (e) `invalidate(id)` clears cache entry so next call hits DB.

Create `apps/platform/audience/test/unit/filter-evaluator.test.ts`: (a) simple `eq` filter matches entity field; (b) `not_within_last 5 days` filter — entity with field 6 days ago returns true, 4 days ago returns false; (c) `EvalContext` is injected (verify `now` is approximately current time by asserting filter-engine was called with a Date object); (d) evaluator passes through filter-engine errors without swallowing.

Create `apps/platform/audience/test/unit/snapshot-manager.test.ts`: (a) first batch (`done: false`) creates snapshot and accumulates matched entities; (b) second batch with same snapshot_id adds to existing, matched_count increments correctly; (c) final batch (`done: true`) seals snapshot to 'ready' and calls enqueueCleanup with 48h delay; (d) entity that fails filter is not included in matched_ids; (e) duplicate entity_id across two batches — `addMembers` ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING means count is not double-incremented; (f) total cap: batch pushing total above 100,000 → throws `SnapshotSizeExceededError`; (g) segment_id mismatch → throws `SegmentMismatchError`.

Create `apps/platform/audience/test/unit/versioning.test.ts`: (a) `PUT` on segment with `active_version IS NULL` → version row overwritten, `current_version` unchanged; (b) `PUT` on segment where `current_version === active_version` → new version row created, `current_version` incremented; (c) `activate` sets `active_version = current_version` and `status = 'active'`; (d) `disable` sets `status = 'disabled'`.
All unit tests use `vi.fn()` mocks for DB and external dependencies — no real DB or Redis.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Use `vi.fn()` and `vi.spyOn()` from Vitest for mocking. The snapshot-manager tests inject a mock `enqueueCleanup` function and verify it's called with correct arguments. For the cache TTL test, override `Date.now` or set `expiresAt` directly on the private cache map (cast to `any` if needed). The versioning tests test the route handler logic — can either call the handler directly with mock db or test the SegmentsRepository methods individually.

userStories.title: Audience Engine — integration tests (core flows)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need integration tests against real Postgres and real Redis (BullMQ) verifying the core named segment lifecycle, inline evaluate flows, and membership check so that end-to-end correctness is confirmed.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `apps/platform/audience/test/integration/setup.ts`: export `setupTestDb()` that creates a Knex connection to test DATABASE_URL, runs migrations via `knex.migrate.latest()`, and returns the db instance. Export `teardownTestDb(db)` that drops and recreates schema. Export `setupTestApp()` that calls `buildApp(db, redis, cleanupQueue)` and returns the Fastify instance + deps for cleanup. Use `beforeAll`/`afterAll` hooks.

Create `apps/platform/audience/test/integration/segments.test.ts`: (a) `POST /audiences/segments` with valid filter → 201, segment and version row in DB; (b) `POST /audiences/segments` without filter → 400 JSON:API error; (c) `GET /audiences/segments/:id` for draft → `filter: null, active_version: null`; (d) `PUT /audiences/segments/:id` on unactivated draft → version row overwritten, current_version unchanged; (e) `POST /audiences/segments/:id/activate` → status='active', active_version=1; (f) `GET /audiences/segments/:id` after activate → filter returned; (g) `PUT /audiences/segments/:id` after activate → new version row created, current_version=2; (h) `POST /audiences/segments/:id/disable` → status='disabled'; (i) `GET /audiences/segments?status=active` → returns only active; (j) `GET /audiences/segments?status=active,draft` → returns both; (k) pagination: create 5 segments, `?limit=2&offset=0` returns 2, `?offset=2` returns next 2, total=5.

Create `apps/platform/audience/test/integration/evaluate.test.ts`: (a) full named segment flow: create → activate → evaluate 2 batches (done:false, done:true) → `GET /audiences/snapshots/:id` returns correct entity_ids, matched_count, segment_version, status='ready'; (b) `GET /audiences/snapshots/:id` while status='accumulating' → returns partial entity_ids; (c) inline `snapshot:false` → matched entity_ids returned, no DB row in audience_snapshots; (d) inline `snapshot:true` → snapshot row written with segment_id=null, segment_version=null; (e) same (entity_id, snapshot_id) submitted twice → single member row (no duplicate), matched_count=1; (f) sealed snapshot + subsequent batch → 400; (g) batch >1000 entities → 413; (h) total snapshot cap: accumulate batches until >100,000 matched → 400 SNAPSHOT_SIZE_EXCEEDED; (i) cross-segment snapshot pollution → 400.

Create `apps/platform/audience/test/integration/check.test.ts`: (a) active segment + matching entity → `{ matches: true, segment_version: 1 }`; (b) active segment + non-matching entity → `{ matches: false }`; (c) segment with no active version → 404; (d) disabled segment → 404.

Create `apps/platform/audience/test/integration/inline-edge-cases.test.ts`: (a) `snapshot:false` + `done:false` → 400; (b) `snapshot:true` + missing snapshot_id → 400; (c) inline snapshot filter mismatch across two batches → 400; (d) `GET /health` → 200 `{ status: 'ok' }`.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Integration tests need Postgres and Redis running — use environment variables `TEST_DATABASE_URL` and `TEST_REDIS_URL` (or the same DATABASE_URL/REDIS_URL if set). The test database should use a separate schema or test database (e.g., postgres://localhost/ortho_test_audience). Clean tables between tests using `TRUNCATE audience_segments, audience_segment_versions, audience_snapshots, audience_snapshot_members CASCADE` in beforeEach. For the 100,000 cap test, don't actually insert 100,000 entities — mock the `getMatchedCount` repository method to return 99,999 and submit a batch of 2 matching entities.

userStories.title: Audience Engine — integration tests (snapshot cleanup + error format validation)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need integration tests for the BullMQ snapshot cleanup workers and JSON:API error format validation so that TTL cleanup and error contracts are verified end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `apps/platform/audience/test/integration/cleanup.test.ts`: (a) primary cleanup job: create a snapshot (done:true), manually call `enqueueSnapshotCleanup(queue, snapshotId, 0)` (delay=0 for immediate firing), process the job, assert `GET /audiences/snapshots/:id` returns 404 and audience_snapshot_members rows are deleted (cascade); (b) cleanup job fires on already-deleted snapshot → no error thrown, worker logs info; (c) safety-net sweep: insert a snapshot row directly into DB with `expires_at = NOW() - interval '1 minute'`, trigger the sweep job handler manually (call the job function directly or enqueue with delay=0), assert snapshot deleted.

Create `apps/platform/audience/test/integration/error-format.test.ts`: (a) `POST /audiences/segments` without filter → response body has `errors[0].status='400'`, `errors[0].code='INVALID_REQUEST'`; (b) `GET /audiences/segments/non-existent-id` → `errors[0].status='404'`; (c) `POST /audiences/segments/:id/evaluate` with no snapshot_id → `errors[0].code='MISSING_SNAPSHOT_ID'`; (d) `POST /audiences/segments/:id/evaluate` with >1000 entities → 413; (e) `POST /audiences/segments/:id/activate` with no filter version → `errors[0].code='NO_FILTER_VERSION'`.

Create `apps/platform/audience/test/integration/filter-snapshot-audit.test.ts`: (a) create segment with filter A, activate, run evaluate, then `PUT` segment to update filter to filter B, activate new version — `GET /audiences/snapshots/:id` still returns `filter_snapshot` = filter A (not filter B); (b) new evaluate creates new snapshot with filter B.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: For BullMQ job testing: you can directly call the worker's job processor function by passing a mock job object `{ data: { snapshotId } }` — no need to go through the full BullMQ queue infrastructure. Alternatively use a test Redis and process jobs with `worker.processJob()` if BullMQ exposes that. Keep cleanup tests simple and focused. The filter_snapshot audit test is important — it verifies that the snapshot records the filter at evaluation time, not whatever the segment's filter is later.

userStories.title: @platform/audience-ui — package scaffold + SegmentLibrary component
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the @platform/audience-ui package scaffolded with a SegmentLibrary component that lists named segments so that marketing staff can browse, create, and manage segments.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `packages/@platform/audience-ui/package.json`: name `@platform/audience-ui`, version `0.1.0`, type `module`, main `dist/index.js`, types `dist/index.d.ts`, engines `{ node: '>=24' }`, scripts `{ build: 'tsc', typecheck: 'tsc --noEmit', test: 'vitest run' }`. peerDependencies: `react ^18.0.0`, `react-dom ^18.0.0`. devDependencies: `typescript ^5.0.0`, `@types/react ^18.0.0`, `@types/react-dom ^18.0.0`, `@types/node ^22.0.0`, `vitest ^2.0.0`.

Create `packages/@platform/audience-ui/tsconfig.json`: compilerOptions `{ target: 'ES2022', module: 'NodeNext', moduleResolution: 'NodeNext', strict: true, outDir: 'dist', rootDir: 'src', declaration: true, declarationMap: true, sourceMap: true, esModuleInterop: true, skipLibCheck: true, jsx: 'react-jsx' }`, include `['src/**/*']`, exclude `['node_modules', 'dist']`.
Create `packages/@platform/audience-ui/vitest.config.ts`: `export default { test: { passWithNoTests: true } }`.

Create `packages/@platform/audience-ui/src/types.ts`: export `SegmentStatus = 'draft' | 'active' | 'disabled'`. Export `SegmentSummary { segment_id: string; name: string; status: SegmentStatus; active_version: number | null; current_version: number; updated_at: string }`. Export `FieldDefinition { key: string; label: string; type: 'string' | 'number' | 'boolean' | 'timestamp' | 'array' }`. Export `SegmentBuilderProps { audienceEngineUrl: string; fields: FieldDefinition[]; onSelect?: (segmentId: string) => void; onFetchEntities?: (filter: unknown) => Promise<Record<string, unknown>[]> }`. Export `AudiencePreviewProps { audienceEngineUrl: string; segmentId: string }`.

Create `packages/@platform/audience-ui/src/api.ts`: export `AudienceApiClient` class. Constructor takes `baseUrl: string`. Methods: `listSegments(status?: string): Promise<{ items: SegmentSummary[]; total: number }>` (GET /audiences/segments), `createSegment(name: string, filter: unknown): Promise<{ segment_id: string; version: number }>` (POST /audiences/segments), `activateSegment(id: string): Promise<void>` (POST /audiences/segments/:id/activate), `disableSegment(id: string): Promise<void>` (POST /audiences/segments/:id/disable), `getSegment(id: string): Promise<{ segment_id: string; filter: unknown | null; status: string }>` (GET /audiences/segments/:id). All methods use fetch() with `Content-Type: application/json`.

Create `packages/@platform/audience-ui/src/SegmentLibrary.tsx`: React functional component. Props: `{ client: AudienceApiClient; onSelectSegment: (id: string) => void; onCreateNew: () => void }`. Displays a table of segments with columns: Name, Status, Version, Last Updated, Actions. Actions per row: 'Use' button (calls onSelectSegment), 'Activate' button (shown for draft status), 'Disable' button (shown for active status). 'New Segment' button at top calls `onCreateNew`. Loads segments with `useEffect` on mount. Show loading state, empty state, and error state. Use only standard HTML/inline styles (no Tailwind or external CSS — this is a standalone package).
Create `packages/@platform/audience-ui/src/index.ts`: export `SegmentLibrary`, `AudienceApiClient`, and all types from `./types.js`.
Run `npm install` from `packages/@platform/audience-ui/`
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: No Tailwind in this package — it's a standalone platform component. Use minimal inline styles or HTML-native styles. The component will be consumed by the CRM web app which handles theming. peerDependencies for React — don't bundle React. The AudienceApiClient uses plain fetch() (not axios) to keep zero runtime dependencies. The CRM web app will mount this component and pass its own audienceEngineUrl. No auth token passed here — the assumption is the browser already has a valid JWT in cookies or the fetch calls will be enriched by the CRM shell via interceptors.

userStories.title: @platform/audience-ui — SegmentEditor component with visual filter tree
userStories.description: As marketing staff, I want a visual AND/OR filter tree editor to build segment filter conditions so that I can compose complex audience criteria without writing JSON.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `packages/@platform/audience-ui/src/FilterTree.tsx`: recursive React component. Props: `{ node: FilterNode; fields: FieldDefinition[]; onChange: (node: FilterNode) => void; onRemove?: () => void }`. Renders: GROUP node (AND/OR) shows toggle for AND/OR, list of child nodes recursively, 'Add condition' button, 'Add group' button. LEAF node shows: field selector (dropdown populated from `fields`), operator selector (operators filtered by field type — string: eq/neq/in/not_in/contains/exists/not_exists; number: eq/neq/gt/gte/lt/lte/exists/not_exists; boolean: eq/not_exists; timestamp: eq/before/after/within_last/not_within_last/date_range; array: contains/exists/not_exists), value input (text for string/number, checkbox for boolean, date picker inputs for temporal). NOT node shows toggle and single child. Remove button on each node (calls onRemove). Import FilterNode type from `@platform/filter-engine/src/types.js` or redefine locally if cross-package import causes issues.

Create `packages/@platform/audience-ui/src/SegmentEditor.tsx`: React functional component. Props: `{ client: AudienceApiClient; segmentId?: string; fields: FieldDefinition[]; onFetchEntities?: (filter: unknown) => Promise<Record<string, unknown>[]>; onSave: (segmentId: string) => void; onCancel: () => void }`. State: `name: string`, `filter: FilterNode` (default: `{ op: 'AND', conditions: [] }`), `previewCount: number | null`, `previewError: string | null`, `saving: boolean`. On mount, if `segmentId` provided, load segment via `client.getSegment(segmentId)` and populate name + filter. 'Save' button: if no segmentId, POST /audiences/segments; if segmentId, PUT /audiences/segments/:id. On save success, call onSave(segmentId). Show filter tree via `FilterTree` component. Live preview: if `onFetchEntities` provided, debounce 500ms after filter changes — call `onFetchEntities(filter)` to get sample entities, then POST to `/audiences/evaluate` inline (snapshot:false, done:true) to get count. Display count or inline error message if preview fails.
Export `SegmentEditor` from `packages/@platform/audience-ui/src/index.ts`.

Create `packages/@platform/audience-ui/src/SegmentBuilder.tsx`: top-level component that manages view state ('library' | 'editor'). Props: `SegmentBuilderProps`. Shows SegmentLibrary when view='library', SegmentEditor when view='editor'. 'New Segment' in library → switch to editor (no segmentId). Clicking 'Edit' on a segment → switch to editor with segmentId. Editor onSave/onCancel → return to library. Export `SegmentBuilder` from index.ts.
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The FilterTree is the most complex component here — keep it functional and correct, even if styling is minimal. The debounced preview is implemented with useRef for the timer: `const timerRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null>(null)`. In the filter onChange callback, clear the previous timer and set a new 500ms timeout. If onFetchEntities throws or the evaluate call returns non-2xx, set previewError and clear previewCount — the editor stays functional (error is advisory only per spec).

userStories.title: @platform/audience-ui — AudiencePreview component
userStories.description: As marketing staff reviewing a campaign, I want a read-only component showing a segment's name, filter summary, and estimated audience count so that I can confirm the audience before scheduling a send.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `packages/@platform/audience-ui/src/AudiencePreview.tsx`: React functional component. Props: `AudiencePreviewProps` (`{ audienceEngineUrl: string; segmentId: string }`). On mount: (1) fetch segment via `GET /audiences/segments/:segmentId` → display name and status; (2) if segment is active, display a human-readable filter summary (field + operator + value text, one line per leaf condition). Show loading state while fetching. Show error state if segment not found or fetch fails. Display: segment name as heading, status badge, list of filter conditions rendered as readable text (e.g. 'Pipeline equals new_patient', 'Last Contact not within last 5 days'), total condition count. No edit controls — read-only.
Export `AudiencePreview` from `packages/@platform/audience-ui/src/index.ts`.

Create a human-readable filter summary helper `packages/@platform/audience-ui/src/utils/filter-summary.ts`: export `summarizeFilter(filter: unknown): string[]` — walks the filter tree and returns an array of human-readable strings for each leaf condition (e.g. `['pipeline eq new_patient', 'opted_out eq false']`). Group/NOT nodes are flattened into their children for summary purposes.
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: AudiencePreview is read-only and simple. The filter summary is best-effort human-readable text — no need for a fully polished natural language renderer. The component uses the same AudienceApiClient pattern for fetching (but takes `audienceEngineUrl` directly per SegmentBuilderProps shape — instantiate AudienceApiClient internally using the provided URL). This is the final story — after this, run typecheck across all new packages and services.
